name: Bolivia (Plurinational State of Bolivia / Estado Plurinacional de Bolivia)
summary: Bolivia is an Andean South American mixed-legal-system plurinational state combining Spanish civil-law substantive heritage with constitutionally-recognised indigenous jurisdictional pluralism (Jurisdicción Indígena Originario Campesina). Family-law framework operates under the Family and Family-Procedural Code 2014 (Código de las Familias y del Proceso Familiar, Law 603 of 2014) replacing the 1972 Family Code. Parental authority and child custody are governed by Family Code arts. 35-49. The Supreme Court of Justice (Tribunal Supremo de Justicia) is the apex court for civil and criminal matters; the Plurinational Constitutional Tribunal (Tribunal Constitucional Plurinacional) operates separate constitutional review. The Constitution recognises Indigenous Originary Peasant Jurisdiction (JIOC) as a parallel jurisdiction. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Family Courts (Juzgados de Familia). Psychology profession is regulated through the Colegio de Psicólogos de Bolivia. Bolivia is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the superior-interest-of-the-child standard codified in Niñez y Adolescencia Code 2014 art. 12. Bolivia is non-Hague Convention.

statutory_framework.title: Family and Family-Procedural Code — Parental authority and custody
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Family Code replacing 1972 Family Code. Arts. 35-49 govern parental authority and child custody.

statutory_framework.title: Children and Adolescents Code
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Children and Adolescents Code codifying superior-interest-of-the-child principle aligned with UNCRC obligations.

statutory_framework.citation: Constitution 2009 — Indigenous Originary Peasant Jurisdiction (JIOC) arts. 190-192
statutory_framework.title: Constitution — Indigenous Originary Peasant Jurisdiction
statutory_framework.relevance: Constitutional recognition of Indigenous Originary Peasant Jurisdiction (JIOC) as parallel jurisdiction — structurally distinctive within the corpus.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of Justice (Tribunal Supremo de Justicia)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: Plurinational Constitutional Tribunal (Tribunal Constitucional Plurinacional)
apex_courts.role: Constitutional Tribunal with original jurisdiction over constitutional review.

professional_regulators.name: Colegio de Psicólogos de Bolivia
professional_regulators.role: Federal professional college for psychologists in Bolivia.

anonymisation_convention: Bolivian family-court decisions are anonymised per Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Original Family Code 1972
key_developments.description: Federal Family Code enacted under military government.

key_developments.title: Plurinational Constitution + JIOC recognition
key_developments.description: Plurinational Constitution adopted recognising Indigenous Originary Peasant Jurisdiction (JIOC) as parallel jurisdiction — structurally distinctive within the corpus.

key_developments.title: Family Code (Law 603) + Niñez y Adolescencia Code (Law 548)
key_developments.description: Comprehensive Family Code replacing 1972 statute and Children and Adolescents Code aligned with UNCRC obligations enacted.

structural_findings: Bolivia operates a structurally distinctive mixed-legal-system framework — Spanish civil-law substantive + constitutionally-recognised Indigenous Originary Peasant Jurisdiction (JIOC) as parallel jurisdiction. Most pluri-jurisdictional indigenous-recognition framework within the corpus.
Plurinational State constitutional designation (2009) is structurally distinctive within the corpus.
Non-Hague Convention status places Bolivia in the non-Hague Andean cluster alongside Ecuador.
editorial_notes: Bolivia jurisdiction sidecar — mixed-legal-system Andean (Spanish civil-law substantive + Indigenous Originary Peasant Jurisdiction (JIOC) constitutional recognition). Family Code 2014 + Niñez y Adolescencia Code 2014 + Constitution 2009 JIOC + non-Hague Convention.
PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator.
Joins Andean + civil-law + Indigenous-jurisdiction-constitutional-recognition distinctive cluster + non-Hague Convention clusters within the corpus.
